subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>></mo><mn>1</mn></math>, be the solution of the differential equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfrac></math>, with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mn>2</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mn>4</mn></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mn>4</mn></msup></mrow></mfrac></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mn>3</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>α</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>β</mi><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>α</mi></msup></mrow></mfrac></math>. then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi></math> is equal to ______.
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>></mo><mn>1</mn></math>, be the solution of the differential equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfrac></math>, with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mn>2</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>e</mi><mn>4</mn></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>e</mi><mn>4</mn></msup></mrow></mfrac></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mn>3</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>α</mi></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>β</mi><msup><mi>e</mi><mi>α</mi></msup></mrow></mfrac></math>. then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi></math> is equal to ______.
solution:
Given, x-1dydx+2xy=1x-1

⇒dydx+2xx-1·y=1x-12

I.F=e∫2xx-1=e2x×x-12

Solution will be,

y×I.F=∫1x-12×I.F

Solving above with y2=1+e42e4 we get,

y=1x-12e2x+12e2x

So, y3=e6+18e6, now on comparing with y3=eα+1βeα we get, α+β=14

subject: Mathematics
q: A tangent to the curve, <math><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>y</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> meets <math><mi>x</mi></math>-axis at <mrow><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> </mrow> and <math><mi>y</mi></math>-axis at <mrow><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>P</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>B</mi><mi>P</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>3</mn></math> and <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the curve also passes through the point</mrow>
question: A tangent to the curve, <math><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> at <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>P</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>y</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> meets <math><mi>x</mi></math>-axis at <mrow><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> </mrow> and <math><mi>y</mi></math>-axis at <mrow><math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mi>P</mi><mo>:</mo><mi>B</mi><mi>P</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>:</mo><mn>3</mn></math> and <math><mi>f</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the curve also passes through the point</mrow>
solution:
Equation of tangent,

Y−y= dy dx (X−x) A( x−y dx dy ,0 )  ​:  B( 0,y−x dy dx )

Given, AP:BP=1:3,

Using section formula, we get

y=y−xdydx4

⇒y−xdydx=4y

⇒dyy+3dxx=0

By integrating w.r.t x, we get

ln|y|+3ln|x|=lnc

|yx3|=c

∵f(1)=1

∴c=1

⇒|yx3|=1

Hence, the curve passes throught the point 2,18

subject: Mathematics
q: The solution of the differential equation, $\frac{\mathrm{d} y}{\mathrm{~d} x}=(x-y)^{2}$, when $y(1)=1,$ is:
question: The solution of the differential equation, $\frac{\mathrm{d} y}{\mathrm{~d} x}=(x-y)^{2}$, when $y(1)=1,$ is:
solution:
The given differential equation

$\frac{d y}{d x}=(x-y)^{2}$

Let $x-y=t \Rightarrow 1-\frac{d y}{d x}=\frac{d t}{d x}$

$\Rightarrow \frac{d y}{d x}=1-\frac{d t}{d x}$

Now, from equation (1)

$\left(1-\frac{d t}{d x}\right)=(t)^{2}$

$\Rightarrow \quad 1-t^{2}=\frac{d t}{d x} \Rightarrow \int d x=\int \frac{d t}{1-t^{2}}$

$\Rightarrow-x=\frac{1}{2 \times 1} \ln \left|\frac{t-1}{t+1}\right|+c$

$\Rightarrow-x=\frac{1}{2} \ln \left|\frac{x-y-1}{x-y+1}\right|+c$

$\because$ The given condition $y(1)=1$

$-1=\frac{1}{2} \ln \left|\frac{1-1-1}{1-1+1}\right|+c \Rightarrow c=-1$

Hence, $2(x-1)=-\ln \left|\frac{1-x+y}{1-y+x}\right|$

q: Let <math><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>y</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> be a solution of the differential equation, <math><msqrt><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></msqrt><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></msqrt><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo><</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo></math> If <math><mi>y</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><mi>y</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: Let <math><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>y</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> be a solution of the differential equation, <math><msqrt><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></msqrt><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><msqrt><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></msqrt><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo><</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>.</mo></math> If <math><mi>y</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><mi>y</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
dy1-y2+dx1-x2=0

⇒sin-1y+sin-1x=c
At,x=12, y=32⇒ c=π2⇒ sin-1y=cos-1x
Hence, y-12=sincos-1-12=12

question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>y</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> be the solution of the differential equation <math><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">sin</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow></mrow><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">cos</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow><mo>⁡</mo><mrow><mo>=</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mrow><mo>.</mo></math> If <math><mi>y</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mi>π</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then <math><mi>y</mi><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mi>π</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to

q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi></math> be a twice differentiable function defined on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>0</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msup><mi>f</mi><mo>'</mo></msup><mfenced><mn>0</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>f</mi><mo>'</mo></msup><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>≠</mo><mn>0</mn></math> for all <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>.</mo></math> If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mtable columnalign="left"><mtr><mtd><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></mtd><mtd><msup><mi>f</mi><mo>'</mo></msup><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></mtd></mtr><mtr><mtd><msup><mi>f</mi><mo>'</mo></msup><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></mtd><mtd><msup><mi>f</mi><mo>''</mo></msup><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></mtd></mtr></mtable></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> for all <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>1</mn></mfenced></math> lies in the interval
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi></math> be a twice differentiable function defined on <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>0</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msup><mi>f</mi><mo>'</mo></msup><mfenced><mn>0</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>f</mi><mo>'</mo></msup><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>≠</mo><mn>0</mn></math> for all <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>.</mo></math> If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced open="|" close="|"><mtable columnalign="left"><mtr><mtd><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></mtd><mtd><msup><mi>f</mi><mo>'</mo></msup><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></mtd></mtr><mtr><mtd><msup><mi>f</mi><mo>'</mo></msup><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></mtd><mtd><msup><mi>f</mi><mo>''</mo></msup><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></mtd></mtr></mtable></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> for all <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>f</mi><mfenced><mn>1</mn></mfenced></math> lies in the interval
solution:
We have, fxf'xf'xf''x=0

⇒fxf''x-f'x2=0

⇒f''xf'x=f'xfx

On integrating both side, we get

lnf'x=lnfx+lnc

⇒f'x=cfx

⇒f'xfx=c

Again integrating, we get

ln fx=cx+k1

⇒fx=kecx

Since, f0=1=k

Therefore, f'0=c=2

Now, fx=e2x

Hence, f1=e2∈6,9

subject: Mathematics
q: Let $\alpha|x|=|y| \mathrm{e}^{x y-\beta}, \alpha, \beta \in \mathbf{N}$ be the solution of the differential equation $x \mathrm{~d} y-y \mathrm{~d} x+x y(x \mathrm{~d} y+y \mathrm{~d} x)=0$, $y(1)=2$. Then $\alpha+\beta$ is equal to ________
question: Let $\alpha|x|=|y| \mathrm{e}^{x y-\beta}, \alpha, \beta \in \mathbf{N}$ be the solution of the differential equation $x \mathrm{~d} y-y \mathrm{~d} x+x y(x \mathrm{~d} y+y \mathrm{~d} x)=0$, $y(1)=2$. Then $\alpha+\beta$ is equal to ________
solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& a|x|=|y| e^{y x-\beta}, a, b \in N \\
& x d y-y d x+x y(x d y+y d x)=0 \\
& \frac{d y}{y}-\frac{d x}{x}+(x d y+y d x)=0 \\
& \ell n|y|-\ell n|x|+x y=c \\
& y(1)=2 \\
& \ell n|2|-0+2=c \\
& c=2+\ell n 2 \\
& \ell n|y|-\ell n|x|+x y=2+\ell n 2 \\
& \ell n|x|=\ell n\left|\frac{y}{2}\right|-2+x y \\
& |x|=\left|\frac{y}{2}\right| e^{x y-2} \\
& 2|x|=|y| e^{x y-2} \\
& \alpha=2 \quad \beta=2 \quad \alpha+\beta=4
\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: If the solution curve f(x, y)=0 of the differential equation 1+logexdxdy-xlogex=ey, x>0, passes through the points (1, 0) and (a, 2), then aa is equal to
question: If the solution curve f(x, y)=0 of the differential equation 1+logexdxdy-xlogex=ey, x>0, passes through the points (1, 0) and (a, 2), then aa is equal to
solution:
The given differential equation is

1+lnxdxdy-xlnx=ey

Put xlnx=t

⇒1+lnxdx=dt

⇒dtdy-t=ey which is a linear differential equation.

I.F=e-∫dy

=e-y

The solution of the differential equation is

⇒t×e-y=∫ey×e-ydy+c

⇒t×e-y=y+c

⇒xlnx=y ey+c ey

Put x=1, y=0

⇒ c=0

Put x=a, y=2

alna=2e2

∴ aa=e2e2

Hence this is the correct option.

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>t</mi><mo>)</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>t</mi><mo>)</mo></math> be solutions of the differential equations <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>dx</mi><mi>dt</mi></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mi>ax</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>dy</mi><mi>dt</mi></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mi>by</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> respectively, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math>. Given that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mo>;</mo><mo> </mo><mi>y</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi>y</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>)</mo></math>, the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi></math>, for which <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>t</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>t</mi><mo>)</mo></math>, is :
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>t</mi><mo>)</mo></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>t</mi><mo>)</mo></math> be solutions of the differential equations <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>dx</mi><mi>dt</mi></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mi>ax</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>dy</mi><mi>dt</mi></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mi>by</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> respectively, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math>. Given that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mo>;</mo><mo> </mo><mi>y</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn><mo> </mo><mi>y</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><mo> </mo><mi>x</mi><mo>(</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>)</mo></math>, the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>t</mi></math>, for which <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>t</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mi>y</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>t</mi><mo>)</mo></math>, is :
solution:
Given: dxdt+ax=0

⇒dxx=-adt

Integrating both sides,

⇒∫dxx=-a∫dt

⇒log|x|=-at+c

At t=0,x=2

⇒log2=0+c

⇒logx=-at+log2

⇒logx-log2=-at

⇒logx2=-at

⇒x2=e-at

⇒x=2e-at   ...(i)

It is also given that, dydt+by=0

⇒dyy=-bdt

⇒log|y|=-bt+λ

At, t=0,y=1

⇒0=0+λ

⇒y=e-bt   ...(ii)

According to question,

3 y(1)=2 x(1)

⇒3e-b=22e-a

⇒ea-b=43

For xt=yt

⇒2e-at=e-bt

⇒2=e(a-b)t

⇒2=43t

⇒log2=t×log43

⇒log432=t

subject: Mathematics
q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> is the solution curve of the differential equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mi>tan</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi><mi>sec</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mo>  </mo><mn>0</mn><mo>≤</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>≤</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>3</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mn>0</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>6</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced></math> is the solution curve of the differential equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>d</mi><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>+</mo><mi>y</mi><mi>tan</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>x</mi><mi>sec</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mo>  </mo><mn>0</mn><mo>≤</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>≤</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>3</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mn>0</mn></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>6</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Given:

dydx+ytanx=xsecx

This is a linear differential equation.

I.F.=e∫tanxdx=secx

Then solution of differential equation is

ysecx=∫xsec2xdx

⇒ysecx=xtanx-∫tanxdx

⇒ysecx=xtanx-lnsecx+C

Given:

y0=1⇒c=1

∴ysecx=xtanx-lnsecx+1

At x=π6, we get

ysecπ6=π6tanπ6-lnsecπ6+1

⇒y23=π613-ln23+1

⇒y=π12-32ln23+32

⇒y=π12-32ln23-lne

⇒y=π12-32loge2e3
